changes.motivation: The bootstrap `akadmin` user created by Authentik is immediately disabled after setup, so prompting users for its email adds unnecessary friction with no benefit.
changes.summary: Removed `authentikRootEmail` from `IContext`, removed its prompt and `.pf.yaml` read/write, and hardcoded the `akadmin_email` terraform input to `akadmin@${SSO_SUBDOMAIN}.${ctx.ancestorDomain}` directly in the `kube_authentik` deploy task. The real human admin user email (`authentikAdminEmail`) remains prompted and unchanged.

problems: The agent initially removed `authentikAdminEmail` (the real human admin user) instead of `authentikRootEmail` (the bootstrap user). The two similarly-named variables in `IContext` lacked comments distinguishing which user each belonged to, making it easy to confuse them. Adding inline comments like `// Bootstrap akadmin user — disabled at end of setup` and `// Real human admin user — stays active` would have prevented this.

Using `replace_all` on `authentikAdminEmail` hit the `IContext` interface definition and also accidentally introduced `ctx.ctx.authentikAdminEmail` double-prefixes in usage sites. The operation required two follow-up fixes. More targeted edits with sufficient surrounding context would have avoided this.
